title: Evaluator Result
description: Per-leaf evaluation block populated on a build_creative variant when the request supplied an advisory `evaluator` and the agent advertises creative.supports_evaluator. EXPLAINS the recommended/rank the agent set on the best_of_n axis. Evaluator identity is established once at the request level and flows to every leaf — implementers MUST NOT re-declare a different evaluator per leaf; each leaf's eval block is scored against the same evaluator the buyer attached to the request. Deliberately mirrors the content-standards oracle shape (validate-content-delivery-response results[] item): a binary verdict plus an optional per-feature breakdown that exposes verdict + rule pointer + directional explanation, never the evaluator's threshold or the submitted creative value. This is pass/fail evaluation and MUST NOT be conflated with creative-feature-result.json, which is a different, closed (additionalProperties:false) schema for quantitative feature MEASUREMENT (value/unit/measured_at). Advisory throughout: the block annotates recommended/rank, it does not change what is produced or billed.

verdict.description: Overall pass/fail verdict the evaluator assigned to this leaf.

features.description: Per-feature breakdown. Oracle pattern: each entry exposes the verdict + an optional rule pointer + a directional explanation, never the evaluator threshold or the submitted creative value. When present, SHOULD include all failed and warning features; MAY include passed features. Mirrors the validate-content-delivery-response results[] feature item field-for-field; it is an inline OPEN item, NOT a reference to creative-feature-result.json.

feature_id.description: Which feature was evaluated (e.g., 'brand_consistency', 'on_brief', 'identity_fidelity'). Evaluator-defined unless drawn from a shared catalog.

policy_id.description: Registry policy ID that triggered this result, when the result originates from a specific registry policy. Enables programmatic routing by looking up the policy in the registry.

explanation.description: Directional human-readable explanation (e.g., 'Headline drifts from the brand voice exemplars'). Avoid quantitative thresholds — the evaluator is the oracle.

confidence.description: Optional evaluator confidence in this result (0-1). Distinguishes certain verdicts from ambiguous ones.

score.description: Optional normalized score for this leaf [0,1]. `rank` (on the variant leaf) remains the cross-leaf ordering of record; score is a finer-grained signal. Evaluators with non-standard output scales (cost functions, perceptual distance) MUST normalize at their own boundary before populating this field.

ranked_against.description: Number of leaves the evaluator scored this leaf against (the best-of-N N). Lets the buyer interpret rank/score in context.

calls_used.description: Number of judge calls made during evaluation. Sellers SHOULD populate when agent_url was used and an eval_budget was supplied, giving buyers visibility into external call usage. No billing coupling in v1.

seconds_used.description: Wall-clock seconds consumed during evaluation. Sellers SHOULD populate when agent_url was used and an eval_budget was supplied, giving buyers visibility into external call usage. No billing coupling in v1.
